market: KOSPI faces significant volatility closing out March, with foreign sell-offs exceeding 30 trillion won. Market sentiment is heavily impacted by geopolitical tensions and the introduction of Google's 'TurboQuant' AI compression algorithm, which sparked concerns over reduced demand for high-end memory chips (Samsung/SK Hynix). Despite institutional exit, individual investors set records for net purchases, betting on recovery.
art: Art market shows K-shaped recovery. Christie's Hong Kong saw a white-glove sale generating $83.8M (+17% YoY), while Art Basel Hong Kong indicates strong interest in Pacific artists. Focus remains on blue-chip names and institutional adoption of AI-integration in new media.
